meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve no marketing skills (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no marketing skills. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Client Workflow to Solve No Marketing Skills
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about marketing. Use this student-focused client workflow to build, ship, and gain experience using Antigravity and Claude Code today.
content.answer_block: You don't need marketing skills; you need a system. Replace cold outreach with a high-value client workflow: identify a niche pain point, build a functional MVP using AI tools, and deliver a demo that solves the problem. By focusing on tangible results, your work markets itself, securing your first professional portfolio project.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio project is daunting, but the real paralyzing fear is the 'marketing void.' You’ve spent hours coding, yet you feel invisible. Most students assume they need a social media presence or a marketing degree to get noticed, but that is a distraction from your true leverage: engineering excellence that solves real-world pain.

True growth isn't found in vanity metrics; it's found in the delivery of a solution so effective that the client asks for more. By adopting a structured, professional client workflow, you turn every project into a business case study. Let's bridge the gap between 'student coder' and 'AI engineer' using the power of Antigravity and Claude Code.

steps.title: Define the High-Value Pain Point
steps.description: Stop building generic apps. Identify a local business or niche community struggling with a repetitive data task. Your goal is to automate one specific bottleneck.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze a small business workflow involving data entry and identify the highest friction point that can be solved with a Python-based AI agent.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project roadmap for a workflow automation script targeting small business data entry.'

steps.title: Architect the MVP Structure
steps.description: Build the foundation. Use Antigravity to scaffold a clean, modular codebase that allows for easy iteration and client feedback.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a production-ready Python project structure with modular handlers for API integration and error logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Initialize directory structure with tests and documentation files.'

steps.title: Develop the Core Logic
steps.description: Implement the core functionality using Claude Code. Focus on clean code over complex features to ensure reliability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust function to process unstructured input into a structured JSON format, including comprehensive error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Implement the data processing function with unit tests to ensure reliability.'

steps.title: Create the Client Demo Interface
steps.description: A demo is worth a thousand tweets. Build a simple, intuitive UI that allows the client to see the 'Before and After' of their workflow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a minimalist FastAPI backend with a simple frontend template to display processed data results.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a simple demo dashboard for the processed output.'

steps.title: Package for Delivery
steps.description: Don't just send code. Package your project as a professional solution with a README that explains the ROI for the client.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional README that highlights the problem solved, the technical stack, and the efficiency gains achieved.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit 'Finalize project documentation and prepare for delivery.'

pro_tips: Never lead with the tech stack; always lead with the time saved for the client.
Use Claude Code to document your 'Why' in every commit message to build a professional audit trail.
The best marketing is a case study: record a 60-second screen capture of your app solving the client's problem.
Build for extensibility—assume the client will ask for a feature addition within 48 hours of the first delivery.
Treat your code comments like documentation for a future developer; it builds habits that recruiters value.
content.next_steps: After completing your first project, record a video walkthrough, upload it to your portfolio, and reach out to one person in that niche to offer a free audit of their workflow.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to stop chasing marketing trends and start building professional equity. By mastering this workflow, you aren't just a student—you are a builder. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
